summary: The current implementation delivers the intended guarded one-command UX in the production-shaped public-CLI E2E and preserves the explicit human boundary, legacy fallback, typed stops, independent review/repair loop, and current-HEAD final gate. One product-contract gap remains: the production implementation owner accepts a completed runtime dispatch whose HEAD did not advance, so a capable provider can silently pass the required implementation-commit step without producing the commit promised by OCR-S-2.
inspection_summary: Inspected the current-head Story, roadmap and predecessor closure records, Architecture, Spec, test plan, public CLI composition and help, run-session/action-owner boundaries, safe-action sequence, production-shaped E2E, owner and Guarded Run tests, persisted dogfood states, current QA evidence, and focused 20-test execution. The happy path, typed stops, review repair, exact-HEAD final prepare, authority boundary, legacy/fallback behavior, and predecessor nonduplication are supported. A same-HEAD completed implementation dispatch remains accepted and has no negative regression fixture.
judgment_delta: Initial concern: the one-command path might be documentation-only or stop before real mutation -> Final conclusion: the public production-owner E2E creates a real managed-worktree implementation commit, performs independent needs_changes review, creates a repair commit, reruns review, and binds final preparation to the repaired HEAD.

Initial concern: autonomy might create or merge a PR, waive a gate, or invoke an external side effect -> Final conclusion: the safe-action plan ends at final_prepare, the owner factory rejects those authority dependencies, and negative spies plus CLI help preserve explicit human operation.

Initial concern: typed-stop and resume coverage might be literal-only -> Final conclusion: executable owner and public-Run scenarios exercise material decision, verification failure, no progress, quota, timeout, CI pending, resume, and cancel; the final acceptance-marker test is redundant rather than the sole evidence.

Initial expectation: a completed implementation dispatch would necessarily prove the required implementation commit -> Final conclusion: src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js passes requireHeadAdvance=false for implement, so an unchanged-HEAD completion advances the DAG without a commit; the current successful E2E does not reject that alternate production result.

findings.detail: OCR-S-2 promises an implementation commit and OCR-T-3 says the production-shaped implementation connector advances the real managed-worktree commit. However, src/one-command-pr-ready-closure.js:105-116 invokes runImplementationRuntime for implement with requireHeadAdvance=false, and runtimeActionResult therefore returns continue when a provider reports completed with the same HEAD. Existing no_progress coverage applies to prepare_artifacts and repair, while the implementation E2E covers only the advancing happy path. Require an implementation HEAD advance or explicitly specify a legitimate already-implemented no-op branch with an independently authoritative completion condition, and add a same-HEAD fixture that would fail before the repair.
